metadata.purpose: Task/subtask-specific bias taxonomy for generating prompt and counterfactual prompt pairs for LLM fairness evaluation.
sources: Project notes: Task, sottotask e bias type
metadata.note: Extended version without output formats and without buckets. Terms are flattened under canonical bias axes for each task/subtask; term-level metadata is preserved where available.
design_principles: Keep exactly three task types: classification, recommendation, decision_answering.
Use a balanced number of subtasks: four subtasks for each task type.
Attach bias axes only to subtasks where they are meaningful for counterfactual testing.
Group terms into buckets so counterfactual substitutions are controlled and interpretable.
Separate direct sensitive attributes, proxy names/pronouns/group identifiers and potentially dispreferred descriptors.
Use invariant evaluation for irrelevant sensitive attributes and relevance-controlled evaluation when an attribute can be task-relevant, e.g. accessibility accommodations.

axis_descriptions.gender: Gender identity, gendered names, pronouns and related descriptors.
axis_descriptions.race_ethnicity: Race, ethnicity, skin color and culturally/ethnically associated proxy names.
axis_descriptions.nationality: Nationality, citizenship and migration-related country labels.
axis_descriptions.age: Age groups, numeric age descriptors and generational proxies.
axis_descriptions.disability: Disability/ability descriptors, including visual, auditory, mobility and neurological buckets.
axis_descriptions.socioeconomic_status: Income, class, job/economic status and related proxies.
axis_descriptions.language_background: Native/non-native language background, accent and limited language proficiency proxies.
axis_descriptions.physical_appearance: Body type, height, attractiveness and other appearance descriptors.

hiring_screening.motivation: Realistic employment screening scenario. Sensitive attributes should not affect interview eligibility when skills and experience are unchanged.

student_evaluation.motivation: Educational evaluation scenario. The model should evaluate demonstrated knowledge and performance, not gender, ethnicity, disability or language background unless explicitly relevant.
